title: Copy and assignment semantics deterministic evidence bank

publication.changeSummary: Added digest-pinned deterministic executable evidence for Copy and assignment semantics; AI-assisted draft remains human-unreviewed and exam-ineligible.

examEligibility.rationale: AI-assisted deterministic draft awaiting independent human standards and assessment review.

privateAuthorNotes: A human reviewer must verify language-version accuracy, distractor validity, equivalence, and grading evidence before formal use.
items.title: Copy and assignment semantics: reject the invalid rule
items.prompt: Which statement correctly resolves this CPP misconception? Compiler-generated copying always performs a deep copy of every resource a class can reach.
hints: Compare each option with the exact language rule, standard boundary, and observable evidence in the authored draft.

feedback.correct: Correct. Generated copying is memberwise; use RAII value members or define deliberate copy semantics when memberwise behavior does not represent an independent value.
feedback.incorrect: That option preserves the documented misconception. Generated copying is memberwise; use RAII value members or define deliberate copy semantics when memberwise behavior does not represent an independent value.

criteria.description: Selects the source-aligned CPP correction instead of the documented misconception.

options.text: Generated copying is memberwise; use RAII value members or define deliberate copy semantics when memberwise behavior does not represent an independent value.
Compiler-generated copying always performs a deep copy of every resource a class can reach.

answer.explanation: Generated copying is memberwise; use RAII value members or define deliberate copy semantics when memberwise behavior does not represent an independent value.

items.title: Copy and assignment semantics: executable application
items.prompt: Create a Value object, copy-construct a second object, change only the copy through a member function, and print original then copy to demonstrate independent value semantics.

examEligibility.rationale: AI-assisted deterministic draft awaiting independent human standards, security, and assessment review.

hints: Begin with the declared input and exact output contract, then implement the named language facet without relying on undefined behavior.

feedback.correct: The reference-aligned visible and hidden deterministic checks passed in the pinned isolated runtime.
feedback.incorrect: At least one deterministic check failed; inspect input validation, boundary behavior, and the named language rule without exposing hidden cases.

criteria.description: Compiles in the pinned runtime and passes every visible and hidden exact-output case.
Uses the target C or C++ language facet with declared bounds and without an out-of-scope workaround.

privateAuthorNotes: AI-assisted and human-unreviewed: formal exam eligibility is prohibited until independent technical and assessment approval.
Reference verification uses the digest-pinned local container with network=none, no package installation, a read-only source mount, and deterministic stdin/stdout.
Executable facet: independent value after copy construction

answer.explanation: The reference solution exercises independent value after copy construction under the bounded deterministic contract; it remains an AI-assisted draft pending human review.
